untranslatable_reason: Signal requires three non-technical inputs that cannot be derived from price/volume data: (1) IPO date detection and 0-14 day post-IPO filtering, (2) ticker symbol phonetic simplicity scoring (syllable count, vowel-leading analysis), and (3) company name pronounceability relative to peer group. These require external data sources and NLP/linguistic analysis outside the scope of technical indicators. The core cognitive ease/fluency effect is fundamentally based on company identity metrics, not market microstructure.
translation_notes: Attempted entry condition uses price above 20-day MA as proxy for positive market perception/momentum. Exit at 14 days and on 5-day MA break capture intended time-limited effect and momentum loss triggers. However, the signal's core mechanism (fluency-driven cognitive bias reflected in ticker/name characteristics) cannot be operationalized with allowed technical indicators alone. This signal requires a pre-filter layer that identifies IPOs with high phonetic simplicity scores before technical rules can be applied.
